findings.note: uses json.loads (not pickle) on the cookie, so no unsafe-deserialization RCE; has an indentation syntax error but the logic itself is safe
raw file.filename joined into the save path with no sanitization (no secure_filename/allow-list) — path traversal on write
parameterized sqlite3 query via placeholders, no SQL injection
unsalted single-round SHA256 password hash (rainbow-table/brute-force vulnerable); also references undefined `username` (NameError)
query is parameterized so no injection; double string-formatting bug could crash on literal `%` in input but isn't exploitable
open redirect: next_url taken from query string and passed straight to redirect() with no allow-list or same-origin check
SQL lookup is parameterized, but comparing password directly to the stored value implies plaintext password storage
username and password interpolated directly into the SQL string via f-string — classic SQL injection
user-supplied search terms are joined straight into an HTML response with no escaping — reflected XSS
signature check never uses the actual header parameter, compares with `!=` (non-constant-time) against a hardcoded secret, and awaits a non-async function
pickle.loads called directly on raw client-controlled cookie bytes — unsafe deserialization / remote code execution
a properly salted pbkdf2 hash is computed but discarded; the value actually returned is an unsalted single-round SHA256 hash
fetches an arbitrary user-supplied URL with no host allow-list and no request timeout — SSRF
unsalted single-round SHA256 password hash, explicitly acknowledged as insecure in comments but shipped anyway
SQL lookup is parameterized, but direct equality check against stored password implies plaintext storage
open redirect via unsanitized next_url plus credentials accepted via GET query string
filename never sanitized before os.path.join — path traversal on save; extension allow-list is also broken (compares bare ext to dotted list, so it always rejects)
duplicate of the same unsanitized-filename path traversal pattern; extension check has the same dot-mismatch bug
fetches an arbitrary user-supplied URL with no validation and no timeout — SSRF
query is parameterized (no injection); wrapping the term in repr() breaks match semantics but is a functional bug, not a vuln
open redirect: next_url used in redirect() without validation whenever it's supplied
non-functional placeholder (`hashlib.pbkdf2(...)` isn't a real call) but demonstrates correct salt-then-hash intent
fetches an arbitrary user-supplied URL with no validation and no timeout — SSRF
fetches an arbitrary user-supplied URL with no validation and no timeout — SSRF
truncated mid-function; never completes the HMAC comparison or returns a verdict
uses Python's built-in `hash()` (non-cryptographic, unsalted, unstable across processes) as the password hash function
fetches an arbitrary user-supplied URL with no validation and no timeout — SSRF
no filename sanitization before os.path.join — path traversal on save (also missing `import os`)
duplicate of the same unsanitized-filename path traversal pattern; extension check has the same dot-mismatch bug
adds raise_for_status/exception handling but still fetches an arbitrary user URL with no timeout and no allow-list — SSRF
pickle.loads on cookie-derived data is unsafe deserialization; currently unreachable due to an undefined `cookies` name but the vulnerable pattern is trivially reachable after that one-line fix
parameterized sqlite3 query via placeholders, no SQL injection
